assertion_results.text: The review comment about transaction wrapping (id 30001) is classified as a code change request and triggers sub-task creation
assertion_results.evidence: review-30001.md classifies comment 30001 as 'code change request' and states 'A sub-task will be created to address this feedback.' report.md shows comment 30001 as 'code change request' with 'Sub-task created'. subtask-30001.md exists with the full sub-task description for transaction wrapping.

assertion_results.text: The review comment about adding an index (id 30002) is classified as suggestion — the reviewer uses suggestive language ('should also', 'would help') and the classification output (review-30002.md) explains this reasoning
assertion_results.evidence: review-30002.md classifies comment 30002 as 'suggestion' and explains the reasoning: '"Should also" implies an enhancement beyond the stated task requirements, not a fix to existing code' and 'The example is introduced with "Something like:" which is tentative, not directive'. The classification output explicitly explains the suggestive language reasoning.

assertion_results.text: The nit comment about context message (id 30003) is classified as nit and does NOT trigger sub-task creation
assertion_results.evidence: review-30003.md classifies comment 30003 as 'nit' and states 'No sub-task created.' report.md shows comment 30003 as 'nit' with 'No sub-task'. No subtask-30003.md file exists in the outputs directory.

assertion_results.text: The question comment about GET behavior (id 30004) is classified as question and does NOT trigger sub-task creation
assertion_results.evidence: review-30004.md classifies comment 30004 as 'question' and states 'No sub-task created.' report.md shows comment 30004 as 'question' with 'No sub-task'. No subtask-30004.md file exists in the outputs directory.

assertion_results.text: Each sub-task description follows the task-description-template.md format: contains Repository, Description, Files to Modify, Implementation Notes, and Acceptance Criteria sections (constraint 1.12)
assertion_results.evidence: subtask-30001.md contains all required sections: '## Repository' (trustify-backend), '## Description' (transaction wrapping), '## Files to Modify' (sbom.rs), '## Implementation Notes' (4 bullet points on transaction implementation), '## Acceptance Criteria' (4 checkboxes). This is the only sub-task created.

assertion_results.text: Each sub-task description includes a Target PR section with the PR URL https://github.com/trustify/trustify-backend/pull/744 (constraint 1.12)
assertion_results.evidence: subtask-30001.md contains '## Target PR' section with the exact URL 'https://github.com/trustify/trustify-backend/pull/744'.

assertion_results.text: Each sub-task description includes a Review Context section with the original review comment text (constraint 1.12)
assertion_results.evidence: subtask-30001.md contains '## Review Context' section with Comment ID 30001, Author reviewer-a, File reference, and the original review comment text quoted: 'The `soft_delete` method should run all three UPDATE statements inside a single database transaction...'

assertion_results.text: The skill does NOT modify any code files — it only reads, verifies, creates sub-task descriptions, and reports (constraint 1.11)
assertion_results.evidence: The outputs directory contains only: report.md, review-30001.md, review-30002.md, review-30003.md, review-30004.md, and subtask-30001.md. All are verification/classification reports or sub-task descriptions. No source code files (.rs, .toml, etc.) were created or modified.

assertion_results.text: The skill reads and processes all PR review comments before generating findings (constraint 1.10)
assertion_results.evidence: report.md states '4 review comments were classified' and lists all 4 comments (30001, 30002, 30003, 30004) in the Review Feedback table. Individual classification files exist for all 4 comments: review-30001.md, review-30002.md, review-30003.md, review-30004.md.

assertion_results.text: The report does NOT auto-merge the PR (constraint 1.13)
assertion_results.evidence: report.md presents findings for human review with an overall verdict of FAIL. No merge action is taken. The report footer states 'This comment was AI-generated by sdlc-workflow/verify-pr v0.11.0' indicating it is a review comment, not a merge action.

assertion_results.text: The report contains a Test Change Classification row with ADDITIVE — only new test files were added (tests/api/sbom_delete.rs is a new file)
assertion_results.evidence: report.md summary table contains: '| Test Change Classification | ADDITIVE | `tests/api/sbom_delete.rs` is a new test file — purely additive |'. The detailed section states: 'tests/api/sbom_delete.rs is a new test file (does not exist on the base branch). It adds 5 new test functions covering the soft-delete endpoint behavior. No existing test files were modified or deleted. This is purely additive test coverage.'

assertion_results.text: Convention upgrade eligibility is evaluated for review comment 30002 (index suggestion) — the review classification output (review-30002.md) or the report's Style/Conventions analysis explains whether the suggestion matches a documented or demonstrated project convention
assertion_results.evidence: review-30002.md contains a '### Convention Upgrade Check' section that explicitly evaluates upgrade eligibility. It states: 'No upgrade to code change request is warranted' and provides 4 reasons: (1) no CONVENTIONS.md content available, (2) no documented project convention backs index creation, (3) no established codebase pattern in the PR diff, (4) general database best practices are insufficient grounds for upgrade.

assertion_results.text: Review comment 30002 (index suggestion) does NOT result in a sub-task — the suggestion classification is correct (suggestive language, no directive) and no project convention in the fixture data backs an upgrade from suggestion to code change request
assertion_results.evidence: review-30002.md classifies as 'suggestion' with 'No sub-task created.' report.md shows comment 30002 as 'suggestion' with 'No sub-task (no convention backs upgrade)'. No subtask-30002.md file exists in the outputs directory. The convention upgrade check in review-30002.md confirms no convention-backed upgrade.

assertion_results.text: Eval Quality is N/A because no eval result reviews exist in the PR — the 3-criteria detection (author github-actions[bot], marker ## Eval Results, footer sdlc-workflow/run-evals) found no matches, so Eval Quality does not affect the Test Quality combination
assertion_results.evidence: report.md Test Quality row states 'Eval Quality: N/A'. The Test Quality detailed section states: 'Eval Quality: N/A — no eval result reviews found on the PR.' Test Quality verdict is PASS (not affected by Eval Quality). Eval Quality is N/A confirming no eval result reviews were detected.
